Option B is correct because it provides another reason that supports the point: learning new words helps your brain grow. Option C is incorrect because it describes a physical feature of books (hard covers) rather than a reason why reading is good for your brain. Option D is incorrect because it tells where books are stored rather than explaining a benefit of reading.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph.

School gardens help students learn. They teach kids how plants grow from seeds. Students can watch roots and leaves appear. Gardens also keep students active. Some students water the plants every morning.

The author says that school gardens help students learn. Which sentences give reasons that support this point? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: They teach kids how plants grow from seeds.
answer_options.B: Students can watch roots and leaves appear.
answer_options.C: Gardens also keep students active.
answer_options.D: Some students water the plants every morning.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it gives a reason that supports the point that school gardens help students learn: gardens teach about plant growth. Option B is correct because it gives another reason for the same point: students can observe roots and leaves to learn. Option C is incorrect because it states a different point (that gardens keep students active), not a reason for the learning point. Option D is incorrect because it supports the other point about being active (watering plants involves movement), not the point about learning.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph below. Then answer the question.

"It is important to wash your hands with soap and water. Washing hands removes germs that can make you sick. Clean hands also keep germs from spreading to other people when you touch things. Some soaps smell like flowers or fruit."

The author states that washing your hands with soap and water is important. Which sentences from the paragraph give reasons that support this point? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Washing hands removes germs that can make you sick.
answer_options.B: Clean hands also keep germs from spreading to other people when you touch things.
answer_options.C: Some soaps smell like flowers or fruit.
answer_options.D: Water comes out of the faucet when you turn the handle.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it explains that handwashing removes germs that cause sickness, which supports the author's point that washing hands is important for your health. Option B is correct because it provides the reason that clean hands prevent spreading germs to others, which also supports why handwashing is important. Option C is incorrect because it only describes a feature of some soaps and does not explain why washing hands is important. Option D is incorrect because it describes how a faucet works rather than giving a reason why handwashing is important.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two texts about butterflies.

Text 1: Butterflies start as tiny eggs. The eggs hatch into caterpillars. Caterpillars eat leaves every day. When they are ready, they form a chrysalis and grow wings.

Text 2: Butterflies begin as small eggs. The eggs open and caterpillars come out. These caterpillars munch on plants. Later, the caterpillar becomes a butterfly with wings.

Which sentences tell something true about the two texts? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Both texts tell that butterflies start as eggs.
answer_options.B: Both texts tell that caterpillars eat plants.
answer_options.C: Text 1 tells that caterpillars eat leaves, but Text 2 tells that caterpillars eat fruit.
answer_options.D: Only Text 1 tells that butterflies form a chrysalis.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Text 1 says butterflies 'start as tiny eggs' and Text 2 says they 'begin as small eggs.' Option B is correct because Text 1 says caterpillars 'eat leaves' and Text 2 says they 'munch on plants,' which means the same thing. Option D is correct because only Text 1 mentions forming a 'chrysalis,' while Text 2 says the caterpillar 'becomes a butterfly' without naming the chrysalis. Option C is incorrect because Text 2 states that caterpillars munch on 'plants,' not fruit, so it does not contrast with Text 1 in the way the option suggests.
subject: ELA

question:
Text 1: Polar bears live in the cold Arctic. They have thick white fur that blends in with ice and snow. These bears hunt seals and fish in the ocean. They are excellent swimmers.

Text 2: Brown bears live in forests and mountains. They have brown fur that helps them hide among trees and dirt. These bears eat berries, nuts, and fish. They climb trees to find food and stay safe.

Read the two texts about bears. Which sentences correctly compare or contrast important points from both texts? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Both texts tell that bears eat fish.
answer_options.B: Text 1 explains that bears have white fur, but Text 2 explains that bears have brown fur.
answer_options.C: Both texts tell that bears climb trees.
answer_options.D: Text 1 explains that bears swim in the ocean, but Text 2 explains that bears climb trees.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Text 1 states that polar bears hunt 'fish in the ocean' and Text 2 states that brown bears 'eat berries, nuts, and fish,' showing a similarity between the two bears. Option B is correct because Text 1 describes 'white fur' while Text 2 describes 'brown fur,' presenting a contrast in appearance. Option D is correct because Text 1 notes that polar bears are 'excellent swimmers' in the ocean while Text 2 notes that brown bears 'climb trees,' showing a difference in how they move. Option C is incorrect because only Text 2 mentions climbing trees; Text 1 describes swimming, not climbing.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these two texts about frogs.

Text 1: Frogs begin life as tiny eggs in the water. When the eggs hatch, the babies are called tadpoles. Tadpoles have tails and swim underwater. Later, they grow legs and can hop onto land.

Text 2: A frog is an animal that lives near ponds and lakes. Young frogs are called tadpoles, and they swim in the water. When frogs are fully grown, they use their long tongues to catch bugs to eat.

Which statements correctly compare important points from the two texts? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Both texts tell that baby frogs are called tadpoles.
answer_options.B: Both texts tell that frogs use their tongues to catch bugs.
answer_options.C: Text 1 explains how tadpoles grow legs, but Text 2 explains how frogs catch food.
answer_options.D: Both texts tell that frogs lay eggs on leaves.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Text 1 and Text 2 identify the babies as tadpoles; Text 1 states that 'the babies are called tadpoles' and Text 2 states that 'Young frogs are called tadpoles.' Option C is correct because it accurately contrasts the important points presented in each text; Text 1 focuses on how tadpoles develop legs to become frogs, while Text 2 focuses on how adult frogs catch their food.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Tom and his dog Max went to the park on Sunday. They played fetch with a red ball. Tom threw the ball, and Max ran fast to catch it.

Which details from the story tell WHO is in the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Tom
answer_options.B: Max
answer_options.C: The park
answer_options.D: Sunday

answer_explanation: Tom and Max are the characters in the story, which answers the question 'who.' Option C is incorrect because 'The park' tells where the story happens, not who is in it. Option D is incorrect because 'Sunday' tells when the story happens, not who is in it.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Lily planted seeds in her garden. She watered them every day. One morning, she saw the leaves turning yellow. "I need to fix this," she thought. She remembered that her grandmother told her plants need sunshine. Lily moved the pots to the sunny window. Soon, the leaves turned green again.

Which details from the story help answer the question: WHY did Lily move the pots to the window? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The leaves were turning yellow.
answer_options.B: Her grandmother told her plants need sunshine.
answer_options.C: She watered them every day.
answer_options.D: The leaves turned green again.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the yellow leaves showed Lily there was a problem, which is the reason she decided to move the pots. Option B is correct because remembering her grandmother's advice about sunshine gave Lily the specific reason to move the pots to the window. Option C is incorrect because it describes what Lily did before the problem happened, not why she moved the pots. Option D is incorrect because it describes what happened after she moved the pots (the result), not the cause or reason for moving them.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Mia went to her backyard on Tuesday afternoon. The sun was shining brightly. She planted flower seeds in the dirt because she wanted to see them grow tall and colorful.

Which details from the story are true? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Mia planted flower seeds.
answer_options.B: Mia was in her backyard.
answer_options.C: It was raining on Tuesday afternoon.
answer_options.D: Mia wanted to hide the seeds in the dirt.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states that Mia planted flower seeds in the dirt. Option B is correct because the story says Mia went to her backyard. Option C is incorrect because the story mentions the sun was shining brightly, not that it was raining. Option D is incorrect because the story states Mia wanted to see the flowers grow, not hide them.
subject: ELA

question:
Tom was excited to build something new. First, he gathered all his colorful blocks. Next, he stacked them one by one to make a tall tower. Then, he placed his toy car on top. Finally, he knocked the tower down and laughed.

Which things did Tom do while playing with his blocks? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: He gathered his colorful blocks.
answer_options.B: He stacked blocks to make a tall tower.
answer_options.C: He placed his toy car on top.
answer_options.D: He put the blocks away in the box.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story states that first, Tom gathered all his colorful blocks. Option B is correct because the story states that next, he stacked them one by one to make a tall tower. Option C is correct because the story states that then, he placed his toy car on top. Option D is incorrect because the story says Tom knocked the tower down at the end, not put the blocks away in the box.
subject: ELA

question: Read the passage. When the first snow fell, Robin noticed that the birds in her backyard looked cold and hungry. She remembered that her grandmother used to hang pinecones covered in peanut butter and seeds. Robin found some string, pinecones from the yard, and a jar of peanut butter. She carefully spread the sticky butter all over the rough cones and rolled them in birdseed. The next morning, Robin watched from her window as bright cardinals and chirping sparrows pecked at her homemade treats. She smiled knowing the birds would have enough energy to stay warm during the long winter. Which events happened in the middle of the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Robin noticed the birds looked cold and hungry.
answer_options.B: Robin found some string, pinecones, and a jar of peanut butter.
answer_options.C: Robin spread peanut butter on the cones and rolled them in birdseed.
answer_options.D: Robin watched birds pecking at her treats the next morning.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because this sentence describes Robin gathering the necessary materials to help the birds, which occurs after she notices the problem and before she creates the solution. Option C is correct because this sentence describes the main action of making the bird treats, which happens after the materials are gathered and before the results are seen the next morning.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Maya was excited for her first day at the new school. She packed her blue backpack with crayons and a notebook. Her mom walked her to the bus stop. The big yellow bus arrived, and Maya climbed the steps. She found a seat next to a girl who was reading a book about dragons. When they got to school, the teacher smiled and showed Maya where to hang her coat.

Which events happened in the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya packed crayons and a notebook in her backpack.
answer_options.B: Maya rode her bike to school with her mom.
answer_options.C: Maya sat next to a girl reading about dragons.
answer_options.D: The teacher showed Maya where to hang her coat.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that Maya packed her blue backpack with crayons and a notebook. Option C is correct because the text says she found a seat next to a girl who was reading a book about dragons. Option D is correct because the text states that the teacher showed Maya where to hang her coat. Option B is incorrect because Maya took the bus to school, not a bike. The text explains that her mom walked her to the bus stop and Maya climbed onto the big yellow bus.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the fable.

A little mouse found a lion sleeping under a tree. The lion woke up and caught the mouse. "Please let me go," said the mouse. "I may help you someday!" The lion laughed but let the mouse go free. A few days later, the lion was caught in a hunter's net. The mouse heard his loud roar and ran to help. She chewed through the ropes and set the lion free.

What is the lesson of this story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Even small friends can be very helpful.
answer_options.B: Kindness shown to others may be returned to you.
answer_options.C: The lion was caught in a hunter's net.
answer_options.D: Lions are bigger and stronger than mice.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story shows the small mouse helping the big lion, teaching that size does not limit helpfulness. Option B is correct because the lion showed kindness by letting the mouse go, and the mouse later returned that kindness by freeing the lion. Option C is incorrect because it states a detail from the story rather than the lesson or moral. Option D is incorrect because it is a true fact from the story but not the central message or lesson.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. ... Which sentences best tell the lesson of this story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Even small friends can be a big help.
answer_options.B: It is unkind to laugh at others.
answer_options.C: A kindness shown today may be returned tomorrow.
answer_options.D: You should only help friends who can help you back.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the story shows the tiny mouse helping the big lion escape from the net, proving that small creatures can help in big ways. Option C is correct because the lion showed kindness by letting the mouse go free, and later the mouse returned that kindness by saving him. Option B is incorrect because while the lion laughed at the mouse, the main lesson of the story is not about laughing being unkind, but about how even small friends can be helpful. Option D is incorrect because the lion helped the mouse without expecting anything in return, and the story teaches that helping others is good regardless of whether they can help you back.
subject: ELA

question:
One sunny day, a tiny mouse accidentally ran across a sleeping lion's paw. The lion woke up and caught the mouse in his sharp claws. 'Please let me go,' squeaked the mouse. 'Maybe someday I can help you!' The lion laughed because the mouse was so small, but he let her go. A few days later, the lion got caught in a hunter's net. He roared loudly for help. The little mouse heard him, ran to the net, and chewed through the ropes with her tiny teeth. The lion escaped and thanked the mouse.

Which lessons does this story teach? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Even small friends can be helpful.
answer_options.B: It is important to keep a promise.
answer_options.C: Being kind to someone may help you later.
answer_options.D: Strong animals do not need help from others.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the tiny mouse used her small teeth to chew through the net and help the big lion escape, showing that size does not limit helpfulness. Option C is correct because the lion showed kindness by letting the mouse go free, and later that kindness was returned when the mouse saved him from the net. Option B is incorrect because although the mouse offered to help, the story focuses on the unexpected value of small friends and the benefits of kindness, not on keeping promises. Option D is incorrect because the strong lion clearly needed help from the small mouse to escape the net, proving that everyone needs help sometimes.
subject: ELA

question: Maya was flying her new kite in the park. Suddenly, the wind blew hard and the kite got stuck high in a tree. Maya felt sad and worried. She looked up at the kite. Then, she asked her dad for help. Together, they used a long stick to get the kite down. Based on the story, how does Maya respond when her kite gets stuck in the tree? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: She feels sad and worried.
answer_options.B: She asks her dad for help.
answer_options.C: She climbs the tree to get the kite.
answer_options.D: She goes home without the kite.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that Maya 'felt sad and worried' when the kite got stuck, showing her emotional response. Option B is correct because the text says Maya 'asked her dad for help,' showing her action to solve the problem. Option C is incorrect because Maya did not climb the tree; instead, she asked her dad for help and used a long stick. Option D is incorrect because Maya did not go home without the kite; she stayed in the park and worked with her dad to get the kite down.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below. Then answer the question.

Jake and Ben were building a tall block tower. They wanted it to touch the ceiling. When the tower was almost done, it crashed to the floor. Ben stomped his foot and crossed his arms tight. Jake took a deep breath and began picking up the scattered blocks. "Let's try making the base wider this time," Jake said. Ben stood still for a moment, then slowly uncrossed his arms and knelt down to help.

Which sentences describe how the characters respond to the challenge of the tower falling? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Ben gives up and walks away from the blocks.
answer_options.B: Jake stays calm and starts cleaning up the fallen blocks.
answer_options.C: Ben shows his frustration by stomping and crossing his arms.
answer_options.D: Jake thinks of a new plan to make the tower stronger.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text states that Jake took a deep breath and began picking up the scattered blocks, showing he stayed calm and took action to address the problem. Option C is correct because the text describes Ben stomping his foot and crossing his arms, which are actions that show he felt frustrated when the tower fell. Option D is correct because Jake suggests making the base wider, which is a new plan to build the tower stronger so it will not fall again. Option A is incorrect because although Ben showed frustration at first, he did not give up or walk away; the text states that he uncrossed his arms and knelt down to help rebuild.
subject: ELA

question:
During the school talent show, Jamal was nervous about singing in front of everyone. When he forgot the words to his song, he paused, took a breath, and started the verse again. After his performance, he told his friend, "I was scared, but I didn't give up."

Which of the following describe how Jamal responds to this challenge? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: He runs off the stage and cries.
answer_options.B: He takes a deep breath and tries again.
answer_options.C: He tells his friend he was scared but didn't give up.
answer_options.D: He asks the teacher to finish the song for him.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the text states that when Jamal forgets the words, he 'paused, took a breath, and started the verse again,' showing he calmed himself and persisted. Option C is correct because after the performance, Jamal tells his friend, 'I was scared, but I didn't give up,' which describes his feelings and his determination to keep going. Option A is incorrect because the story does not say Jamal ran off the stage or cried; instead, he continued singing. Option D is incorrect because Jamal does not ask for someone else to finish; he completes the song himself.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these lines from a poem:

"Rain, rain, go away.
Come again another day."

Which words or phrases supply rhythm and meaning in this poem? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: the repeated words "rain, rain"
answer_options.B: the rhyming words "away" and "day"
answer_options.C: the word "thunder"
answer_options.D: the word "come"

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because repeating the word "rain" creates a steady beat (rhythm) and emphasizes what the speaker wants to go away (meaning). Option B is correct because the rhyme between "away" and "day" gives the lines a musical sound (rhythm) and connects the idea of the rain leaving with the time frame of another day (meaning). Option C is incorrect because "thunder" does not appear anywhere in the poem. Option D is incorrect because although "come" is in the poem, it does not supply the rhythm through repetition, rhyme, or alliteration the way the other choices do.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these lines from the poem 'Rain Song.'

Pitter patter, pitter patter,
Rain upon the roof.
Crash, bang, crash, bang,
Thunder strikes aloof.
Whirling wind, whirling wind,
Trees begin to sway.
Pitter patter, pitter patter,
Rain goes away.

Which phrases use repetition, alliteration, or rhyme to supply both a steady beat (rhythm) AND show what the storm sounds like (meaning)? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Pitter patter, pitter patter
answer_options.B: Crash, bang, crash, bang
answer_options.C: Rain upon the roof
answer_options.D: Whirling wind, whirling wind

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the repeated words 'pitter patter' use alliteration (the 'p' sound) to create a steady, tapping beat that mimics the sound of falling rain, supplying both rhythm and meaning. Option B is correct because the repetition of 'crash, bang' creates a strong, pounding beat that mimics the sound of thunder, giving the line rhythm and helping the reader hear the storm. Option D is correct because the repetition and alliteration of the 'w' sound in 'whirling wind' create a swirling, breezy beat that helps the reader hear the wind blowing. Option C is incorrect because it simply states where the rain is falling but does not use repetition, alliteration, or rhyme to create a rhythmic beat or sound effect.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the poem.

Crash and bang, crash and bang, the drums play loud.
Crash and bang, crash and bang, play for the crowd.

Which words or phrases use repetition or rhyme to supply rhythm and meaning in the poem? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The repeated phrase "Crash and bang"
answer_options.B: The rhyming words "loud" and "crowd"
answer_options.C: The word "drums"
answer_options.D: The word "play"

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the repeated phrase "Crash and bang" creates a regular beat (repeated lines) and is an example of onomatopoeia that mimics the sound of drums, supplying both rhythm and meaning. Option B is correct because the rhyming words "loud" and "crowd" at the ends of the lines create a rhythmic pattern that helps the poem flow. Option C is incorrect because "drums" names the instrument making the sound but does not itself use repetition, rhyme, or alliteration to create rhythm. Option D is incorrect because "play" is simply a verb describing the action and does not contribute to the poem's sound pattern or rhythmic structure.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Tom lost his blue hat on the playground. He looked under the swings and by the slide. Then he found it hiding in a bush.

Which sentences tell what happens at the beginning or the ending of the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Tom lost his blue hat on the playground.
answer_options.B: He looked under the swings and by the slide.
answer_options.C: Then he found it hiding in a bush.
answer_options.D: Tom bought a new red hat at the store.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it describes the beginning of the story, which introduces Tom and the problem (losing his hat). Option C is correct because it describes the ending, which concludes the action by showing the hat is found. Option B is incorrect because it describes a middle event where Tom is searching, not the beginning or ending. Option D is incorrect because it does not happen in the story; Tom finds his lost hat, he does not buy a new one.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Maya looked at the empty garden bed where her sunflower seeds should have sprouted. She had watered them every day for two weeks. Her neighbor Mrs. Lee noticed Maya's frown and brought over a small green plant in a pot. 'This tomato seedling needs a home,' Mrs. Lee said. By August, Maya's garden was full of ripe tomatoes and tall sunflowers that finally bloomed.

Which statements describe how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The first sentence introduces Maya and shows the problem she is facing.
answer_options.B: The last sentence concludes the story by showing what happened to Maya's garden later.
answer_options.C: The story begins with Mrs. Lee giving Maya a tomato plant.
answer_options.D: The ending tells us that Maya watered the seeds every day.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the first sentence introduces the main character Maya and establishes the problem that her sunflower seeds have not sprouted. Option B is correct because the last sentence concludes the action by showing the final result of the garden with both tomatoes and blooming sunflowers. Option C is incorrect because Mrs. Lee giving Maya the plant happens in the middle of the story, not at the beginning. Option D is incorrect because Maya watering the seeds is mentioned in the second sentence as part of the story's background information, not in the ending.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the short story below.

Maya was walking home from school. She saw a lost puppy shivering by the road. Maya wrapped the puppy in her warm scarf. That evening, the puppy licked her hand and fell asleep.

Which sentences show how the beginning introduces the story or how the ending concludes the action? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya was walking home from school.
answer_options.B: She saw a lost puppy shivering by the road.
answer_options.C: Maya wrapped the puppy in her warm scarf.
answer_options.D: That evening, the puppy licked her hand and fell asleep.

answer_explanation: The beginning of a story introduces the characters and setting. Option A is correct because it is the first sentence that introduces the character Maya and where she is. The ending of a story concludes the action. Option D is correct because it is the last sentence that shows how the story ends with the puppy safe and comfortable. Options B and C are incorrect because they describe the middle of the story where the problem is presented and solved.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story.

Ben said, "I love going to the dentist! They give me a sticker and my teeth feel super clean."

Zoe said, "I do not like the dentist. The chair is scary and the tools make loud noises."

Which sentences tell how Ben and Zoe feel about the dentist? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Ben thinks the dentist is fun because he gets a sticker.
answer_options.B: Zoe thinks the dentist is scary because of the loud noises.
answer_options.C: Ben thinks the dentist chair is scary.
answer_options.D: Zoe loves going to the dentist for clean teeth.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Ben says he loves going to the dentist and mentions getting a sticker. Option B is correct because Zoe says she does not like the dentist and mentions the scary chair and loud noises. Option C is incorrect because Ben loves the dentist; he does not think the chair is scary. Option D is incorrect because Zoe does not like the dentist and does not mention clean teeth; she finds the chair scary.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the story below. Think about how each character feels about the kitten. Which sentences describe the different points of view? Select ALL that apply.

Tommy found a small kitten hiding behind the garage. He gently picked it up and held it close. "Look how tiny and soft it is! Mom, can we please keep it?" he asked with a big smile. Mom sighed and crossed her arms. "Tommy, taking care of a pet means trips to the vet and daily feeding. It's a big responsibility for our family," she replied. Tommy's sister Lily stepped outside and peeked at the kitten. "Oh! It has bright blue eyes just like my favorite doll," she whispered. "If we keep it, can I name it Sapphire?"

answer_options.A: Tommy believes the kitten is cute and wants to adopt it as a family pet.
answer_options.B: Mom feels that caring for a pet would require too much effort and money.
answer_options.C: Lily is interested in the kitten because it reminds her of her toy.
answer_options.D: All the characters agree that the kitten should stay in the garage.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that Tommy smiled and asked if they could keep the kitten, showing he thinks it is cute and wants to adopt it. Option B is correct because Mom mentions visits to the vet and responsibility, indicating she is concerned about the effort and cost. Option C is correct because Lily mentions the kitten has eyes like her doll, showing she is interested because it reminds her of her toy. Option D is incorrect because the characters do not agree; Tommy and Lily want to keep the kitten, while Mom is hesitant, and no one suggests it should stay in the garage specifically.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story. Sam looked at the tall slide and whispered, "I don't think I can climb that high. It looks scary up there." Lily ran to the ladder and called back, "Come on! The view from the top is amazing. Let's go together!" Which sentences tell the characters' different points of view about the slide? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Sam feels nervous about climbing the slide.
answer_options.B: Lily feels excited to try the slide.
answer_options.C: Sam wants to run to the ladder immediately.
answer_options.D: Lily thinks the slide is too scary to climb.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Sam whispers and says the slide looks "scary," which shows he feels nervous about climbing it. Option B is correct because Lily runs to the ladder and says the view is "amazing," which shows she feels excited and happy about the slide. Option C is incorrect because Sam says he doesn't think he can climb that high, showing he does not want to run to the ladder immediately. Option D is incorrect because Lily encourages Sam to climb and describes the view positively, showing she does not think the slide is too scary.
subject: ELA

question: Read the story and. The story says: 'Maya smiled as she packed her bag. "I can't wait to see Grandma!" she said.'. A calendar on the wall shows Saturday circled in red, and a bus ticket sits on the table. Which details help you understand the story? Select ALL that apply?

answer_options.A: Maya feels happy
answer_options.B: Maya is going to visit her Grandma
answer_options.C: Maya is at the playground
answer_options.D: It is Saturday

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the words say Maya 'smiled' and the picture shows her looking happy while packing. Option B is correct because the words say she 'can't wait to see Grandma' and the picture shows a bus ticket and packed bag, indicating a trip to visit family. Option D is correct because the picture shows Saturday circled on the calendar. Option C is incorrect because nothing in the words or picture shows a playground; the picture shows Maya packing at home.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the paragraph and look at the illustration.

Text: 'The bus stopped at the corner. Jamal looked out the window and frowned. He picked up his heavy backpack and walked to the door.'

Picture: The illustration shows a boy with a downturned mouth carrying a large red backpack. He is stepping off a bus onto a sidewalk near small houses. There are gray rain clouds in the sky.

Which statements are true based on information from BOTH the words and the picture? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Jamal is getting off the bus.
answer_options.B: Jamal looks unhappy.
answer_options.C: Jamal is carrying something heavy.
answer_options.D: It is a sunny day.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text says Jamal 'walked to the door' and the picture shows him 'stepping off the bus,' which together show he is exiting the bus. Option B is correct because the text states Jamal 'frowned' and the picture shows a 'downturned mouth,' both indicating he looks unhappy. Option C is correct because the text mentions a 'heavy backpack' and the picture shows a 'large red backpack,' confirming he is carrying something heavy. Option D is incorrect because the picture shows 'gray rain clouds' rather than a sunny day.
subject: ELA

question: . What do the picture and the words together tell you about the story? Select ALL that apply. Sentences: "The family was ready for the beach trip. They packed sandwiches and hats. Mom looked up at the sky."?

answer_options.A: The setting is a sunny day at the beach.
answer_options.B: The family might have trouble with the weather.
answer_options.C: The story takes place when dark clouds are in the sky.
answer_options.D: The family did not pack any food.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because the picture shows dark clouds, which suggests bad weather that could cause a problem for the family's beach trip mentioned in the text. Option C is correct because the picture shows dark clouds in the sky, which establishes part of the story's setting. Option A is incorrect because the picture shows dark clouds rather than sunny weather. Option D is incorrect because the text explicitly states that the family packed sandwiches, meaning they did pack food.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these two story summaries.

Story 1: Ella wants to go to a royal ball. A fairy godmother helps her wear glass slippers. She must leave at midnight.

Story 2: Yeh-Shen wants to go to a spring festival. A magical fish helps her wear golden slippers. She must leave at midnight.

Which sentences tell how the two stories are DIFFERENT? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: One girl is helped by a fairy godmother, and the other girl is helped by a magical fish.
answer_options.B: One girl wears glass slippers, and the other girl wears golden slippers.
answer_options.C: Both girls want to go to a party.
answer_options.D: Both girls must leave at midnight.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the stories have different magical helpers—a fairy godmother in Story 1 and a fish in Story 2. Option B is correct because the stories have different slipper materials—glass in Story 1 and gold in Story 2. Option C is incorrect because it describes a similarity (both wanting to go to a party), not a difference. Option D is incorrect because it also describes a similarity (both leaving at midnight), not a difference.
subject: ELA

question:
Story 1: In the French version of Cinderella, a girl named Ella lives with her unkind stepmother and stepsisters. A fairy godmother helps Ella go to a ball at the palace. Ella loses a glass slipper, and the prince uses it to find her.

Story 2: In the Chinese version Yeh-Shen, a girl lives with her unkind stepmother and sister. A magical fish helps Yeh-Shen go to a spring festival. Yeh-Shen loses a golden slipper, and the king uses it to find her.

Read the two summaries. Which sentences correctly compare the two stories? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Both main characters are treated unkindly by family members.
answer_options.B: One character is helped by a fairy godmother, while the other is helped by a magical fish.
answer_options.C: Both stories take place in the same country.
answer_options.D: Both characters lose a slipper that helps a royal person find them.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Ella and Yeh-Shen live with unkind stepmothers and stepsisters. Option B is correct because it identifies a difference: Ella has a fairy godmother while Yeh-Shen has a magical fish. Option D is correct because both girls lose a slipper that leads a prince or king to find them. Option C is incorrect because the stories take place in different countries (France and China).
subject: ELA

question:
Read the two story summaries.

**Version A (from Europe):** A kind girl named Ella lives with her mean stepmother. She wishes to go to the royal ball. A fairy godmother helps her attend, but she must leave before midnight. Ella loses a glass slipper, and a prince uses it to find her.

**Version B (from China):** A kind girl named Yeh-Shen lives with her mean stepmother. She wishes to go to the spring festival. A magical fish helps her attend, but she must leave at night. Yeh-Shen loses a golden slipper, and a king uses it to find her.

Which details are found in BOTH versions of the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A girl is treated unkindly by her stepmother.
answer_options.B: The girl loses a slipper at a celebration.
answer_options.C: A fairy godmother helps the girl.
answer_options.D: The girl marries a prince.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because both Version A and Version B describe a girl who lives with a mean stepmother. Option B is correct because both stories tell about the girl losing a slipper at a special event (the ball in Version A and the festival in Version B). Option C is incorrect because only Version A mentions a fairy godmother; Version B says a magical fish helps the girl. Option D is incorrect because only Version A mentions a prince; Version B mentions a king finding the girl, and the summary does not say she marries him (even if she did, he is a king, not a prince).
subject: ELA

question: During a class discussion about a story, which students are following the discussion rules? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Juan raises his hand and waits to be called on before speaking.
answer_options.B: Emma interrupts her friend to share her idea immediately.
answer_options.C: Aiden looks at the speaker and listens carefully to what is being said.
answer_options.D: Sofia waits for her partner to finish talking before she begins.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because raising a hand and waiting to be called on is a respectful way to gain the floor to speak. Option C is correct because looking at the speaker and listening carefully shows listening to others with care. Option D is correct because waiting for a partner to finish talking follows the rule of speaking one at a time. Option B is incorrect because interrupting a friend violates the rule of speaking one at a time and is not a respectful way to participate.
subject: ELA

question: During a class discussion about the story 'The Three Little Pigs,' four students do different things. Which students are following the agreed-upon rules for discussions? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya raises her hand and waits quietly until the teacher calls on her before sharing her idea about the wolf.
answer_options.B: Jack talks to his neighbor about his weekend plans while another student is explaining why the brick house was strong.
answer_options.C: Sofia blurts out 'I think the straw house was silly!' while the teacher is still asking a question.
answer_options.D: Lucas looks at the speaker and nods to show he is listening carefully while she discusses the characters.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Maya gains the floor in a respectful way by raising her hand and waiting to be called on, and she stays on topic by discussing the wolf from the story. Option D is correct because Lucas demonstrates listening to others with care by looking at the speaker and showing he is paying attention during the discussion. Option B is incorrect because Jack is not listening to others with care and is talking about an off-topic subject (weekend plans) instead of the text under discussion. Option C is incorrect because Sofia is not speaking one at a time; she blurts out her comment while the teacher is still speaking, which violates the turn-taking rule.
subject: ELA

question: During a class discussion about a story, the teacher asks students to share their ideas. Which students are following the rules for a good discussion? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Maya raises her hand and waits until the teacher calls on her before speaking.
answer_options.B: Carlos looks at the student who is talking and stays quiet until she finishes.
answer_options.C: Zoe starts telling her idea while another student is still speaking.
answer_options.D: Leo looks at the ceiling and hums while his classmate shares a thought.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because raising a hand and waiting to be called on is a respectful way to gain the floor during a discussion. Option B is correct because looking at the speaker and staying quiet shows that Carlos is listening to others with care and waiting for his turn to speak. Option C is incorrect because starting to talk while someone else is speaking violates the rule of speaking one at a time. Option D is incorrect because looking away and humming while a classmate speaks shows that Leo is not listening with care to what others are saying.
subject: ELA

question: Maya says, "I saw a robin building a nest in our tree yesterday." Which responses build on Maya's comment by linking to what she said? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What kind of twigs did the robin use?
answer_options.B: I saw a cardinal in my yard last week!
answer_options.C: My favorite color is blue.
answer_options.D: I like to eat pizza for dinner.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it asks a question about the specific details of the robin's nest that Maya mentioned, building on her comment. Option B is correct because it shares a related experience about seeing another type of bird, linking to Maya's remark about the robin. Option C is incorrect because favorite colors have no connection to Maya's comment about the bird and nest. Option D is incorrect because dinner preferences are unrelated to the topic of birds and nests that Maya introduced.
subject: ELA

question:
Read the conversation below.

Tariq: "I think we should clean up the park on Saturday because the wind blew litter everywhere yesterday."

Which responses build on Tariq’s talk by linking their comments to what he just said? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: That's a good idea, and we could bring trash bags and gloves.
answer_options.B: I saw a lot of plastic bottles near the swings.
answer_options.C: I like playing at the park on weekends.
answer_options.D: The wind could have blown litter into the trees too.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it builds on Tariq’s proposal to clean up by adding specific details (bringing supplies) that link directly to his plan. Option B is correct because it links to Tariq’s mention of litter by providing a specific example of where litter can be found, building on his observation. Option D is correct because it connects to Tariq’s comment about the wind blowing litter by extending that idea to another possible location (the trees), showing careful listening and elaboration. Option C is incorrect because it changes the topic to playing at the park rather than addressing Tariq’s specific remarks about cleaning up the wind-blown litter.
subject: ELA

question: Maya says, 'I think we should make a bird feeder for our class garden.' Which replies build on what Maya said? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: That would help birds find food when it snows.
answer_options.B: I saw a red cardinal in my backyard yesterday.
answer_options.C: We could add a small water dish next to it.
answer_options.D: I like to play soccer at recess with my friends.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it links to Maya's remark by explaining why making a bird feeder would be helpful. Option C is correct because it builds on Maya's idea by suggesting an addition to the plan. Option B is incorrect because it mentions seeing a bird but does not respond to or build on Maya's specific suggestion about making a feeder. Option D is incorrect because it changes the topic to playing soccer instead of discussing the bird feeder idea.
subject: ELA

question: During circle time, your teacher says, 'We will study habitats tomorrow.' Which questions ask for clarification or further explanation about the topic? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Can you explain what a habitat is?
answer_options.B: Which animals will we learn about?
answer_options.C: Do we need to bring our pencils?
answer_options.D: Can you tell us more about different habitats?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it asks the teacher to explain the meaning of the word 'habitat,' which is asking for clarification. Option B is correct because it asks for more specific information about what will be studied, which is asking for further explanation about the topic. Option C is incorrect because it asks about school supplies rather than asking for clarification or further explanation about habitats. Option D is correct because it asks for additional details about the topic, which is asking for further explanation.
subject: ELA

question: Your teacher reads aloud: 'The ancient Egyptians built huge pyramids as tombs for their kings. They used massive stone blocks that workers pulled into place.' Which questions ask for clarification or further explanation about what the teacher read? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What does the word tombs mean?
answer_options.B: How did the workers move the massive stones?
answer_options.C: Have you ever seen a pyramid?
answer_options.D: Why are pyramids shaped like triangles?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it asks for clarification of the specific word 'tombs' used in the text. Option B is correct because it asks for further explanation of the building process described. Option C is incorrect because it asks about personal experience rather than clarifying the text. Option D is incorrect because it asks about the shape of pyramids, which was not discussed in the passage, rather than clarifying the information about tombs or building methods.
subject: ELA

question: During a class discussion about a story, your teacher says, 'The main character made a sacrifice to help her friend.' Which questions ask for clarification or further explanation about the story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What does the word 'sacrifice' mean?
answer_options.B: Why did the character decide to help her friend?
answer_options.C: How did the friend feel after receiving help?
answer_options.D: Do you think making sacrifices is a good thing?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it asks for the meaning of a specific word used in the discussion, which is asking for clarification. Option B is correct because it seeks further explanation about the character's reasons and motivations in the story. Option C is correct because it asks for additional details about the story events, seeking further explanation. Option D is incorrect because it asks for a personal opinion rather than clarifying or explaining the story content.
subject: ELA

question: Principal Gomez made an announcement over the speaker. She said that Friday is Field Day. She also reminded students to wear comfortable clothes and to bring a water bottle. What did Principal Gomez announce about Field Day? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Students should wear comfortable clothes.
answer_options.B: Field Day is on Friday.
answer_options.C: Students must bring a lunch from home.
answer_options.D: The event will be held at the park.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because Principal Gomez specifically reminded students to wear comfortable clothes. Option B is correct because she stated that Friday is Field Day. Option C is incorrect because she did not mention bringing lunch from home; she only mentioned bringing a water bottle. Option D is incorrect because she did not say where the event will take place.
subject: ELA

question: Listen to this: Honeybees visit flowers to drink sweet nectar. They carry the nectar in a special stomach back to their hive. Inside, they fan the nectar with their wings to make it dry and thick, turning it into honey. Which sentences tell key details from what you heard? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Bees drink sweet nectar from flowers.
answer_options.B: Bees carry nectar in a special stomach.
answer_options.C: Bees use pollen to make honey.
answer_options.D: Bees fan the nectar with their wings to dry it.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the text states that honeybees visit flowers to drink sweet nectar. Option B is correct because the text explains that bees carry the nectar in a special stomach back to the hive. Option D is correct because the text describes how bees fan the nectar with their wings to make it dry and thick. Option C is incorrect because the text says bees use nectar, not pollen, to make honey; pollen is a different substance bees collect for other purposes.
subject: ELA

question: Our teacher showed us a video about how plants grow. The narrator explained that plant roots grow down into the soil to find water. She also said that green leaves use sunlight to make food for the plant. Which details were explained in the video? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Roots grow downward to find water.
answer_options.B: Leaves use sunlight to make plant food.
answer_options.C: Roots grow upward toward the light.
answer_options.D: Leaves absorb rainwater through their edges.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because the narrator stated that roots grow down into the soil to find water. Option B is correct because the narrator explained that green leaves use sunlight to make food for the plant. Option C is incorrect because the video stated that roots grow downward, not upward toward the light. Option D is incorrect because the video did not mention leaves absorbing rainwater; it explained that roots find water in the soil.
subject: ELA

question: Mrs. Lee tells the class, "Butterflies begin life as eggs, which hatch into caterpillars." Which questions could a student ask to learn more about what Mrs. Lee said? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What happens to the caterpillar next?
answer_options.B: What do caterpillars eat?
answer_options.C: Do butterflies come from eggs?
answer_options.D: Can butterflies fly south for winter?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking what happens next gathers additional information about the butterfly's life cycle that deepens understanding of what the teacher explained. Option B is correct because asking what caterpillars eat gathers specific details about the caterpillar stage mentioned by the speaker. Option C is incorrect because the teacher already stated that butterflies begin as eggs, so this question would not gather new information or clarify the explanation. Option D is incorrect because it asks about migration, which is not related to the topic of how butterflies begin their lives that the teacher was explaining.
subject: ELA

question: During a science presentation, the speaker says: "Butterflies begin life as tiny eggs. When the eggs hatch, caterpillars come out and eat lots of leaves to grow. Once big enough, a caterpillar forms a chrysalis around itself. Inside the chrysalis, the caterpillar changes into a butterfly." Which questions would help you clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen your understanding of what the speaker explained? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What happens inside the chrysalis to turn the caterpillar into a butterfly?
answer_options.B: How long does a caterpillar usually stay inside the chrysalis?
answer_options.C: What is the best way to catch a butterfly with a net?
answer_options.D: Why do caterpillars need to eat so many leaves before they make a chrysalis?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking what happens inside the chrysalis deepens understanding of the transformation process described by the speaker. Option B is correct because asking about the duration gathers additional specific information about the timeline that the speaker did not provide. Option D is correct because asking why caterpillars eat so much clarifies the purpose of that stage and deepens understanding of the life cycle. Option C is incorrect because asking about catching butterflies is unrelated to the speaker's explanation of the life cycle stages and does not help clarify, gather information about, or deepen understanding of the topic presented.
subject: ELA

question: Listen to what the teacher says: 'Frogs start as eggs in the water. The eggs hatch into tadpoles, which have tails and live underwater. Later, the tadpoles grow legs and lose their tails to become adult frogs.' Which questions could you ask to learn more about this topic or understand it better? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: What do tadpoles eat while they are growing?
answer_options.B: Why do tadpoles need to live in water?
answer_options.C: How long does it take for a tadpole to become a frog?
answer_options.D: What is your favorite color of frog?

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because asking what tadpoles eat gathers additional information about their diet that was not mentioned in the teacher's explanation. Option B is correct because asking why tadpoles need water deepens understanding of their habitat requirements and helps clarify the importance of the underwater environment. Option C is correct because asking about the timeline gathers additional information about the life cycle process that would deepen understanding. Option D is incorrect because asking about a favorite color is a personal preference question that does not help clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of the frog life cycle topic the speaker explained.
subject: ELA

question: Liam is telling his class about baking cookies with his grandmother. He wants to include relevant, descriptive details in his story. Which sentences would help Liam tell his experience? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The warm kitchen smelled like sweet vanilla and cinnamon.
answer_options.B: We mixed the dough until it was soft and sticky.
answer_options.C: Thunder is loud because lightning heats the air.
answer_options.D: The cookies were round and had chocolate chips on top.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses sensory words like 'warm' and 'sweet' to describe how the kitchen smelled during the baking experience. Option B is correct because it uses descriptive words like 'soft' and 'sticky' to tell what the dough felt like while they were baking. Option D is correct because it uses descriptive details like 'round' and 'chocolate chips' to describe the cookies they made. Option C is incorrect because it explains a scientific fact about weather that is not related to the experience of baking cookies with a grandmother.
subject: ELA

question: Read this recount of an experience: 'Yesterday, I helped my mom bake cookies for my sister's birthday party. We worked in the kitchen all afternoon.' Which sentences add appropriate facts or relevant, descriptive details to this story? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: The warm cookies smelled like sweet vanilla and melted chocolate chips.
answer_options.B: We baked the cookies for exactly twelve minutes until they were golden brown.
answer_options.C: My grandmother lives in a house that is very far away from our city.
answer_options.D: Cookies are small, flat baked treats that people eat for dessert.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it adds a descriptive sensory detail about how the specific cookies smelled, making the recount more vivid and helping the listener picture the experience. Option B is correct because it provides a relevant, specific fact about the baking process and the appearance of the cookies for this particular experience. Option C is incorrect because it introduces information about a grandmother that is unrelated to the baking experience being recounted. Option D is incorrect because it gives a general definition of what cookies are rather than adding a specific detail about this particular baking experience.
subject: ELA

question: Maya is telling her classmates about her camping trip in the woods. Which sentences add relevant, descriptive details to her story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: The tent smelled like pine needles and fresh rain.
answer_options.B: We roasted ten gooey marshmallows over the crackling fire.
answer_options.C: School starts at eight o'clock in the morning.
answer_options.D: Fish breathe underwater using their gills.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses sensory details about smell (pine needles and fresh rain) to help the audience imagine Maya's camping experience. Option B is correct because it includes appropriate facts (ten marshmallows) and descriptive words (gooey, crackling) that make the story more specific and vivid. Option C is incorrect because it states a fact about school schedules that is not relevant to Maya's camping trip story. Option D is incorrect because it explains a scientific concept about fish that is not a descriptive detail about her personal camping experience.
subject: ELA

question: Jake wrote a story about his birthday party. He wants to share it with his class and help them understand how happy he felt. Which could Jake add to his presentation to clarify his ideas, thoughts, and feelings? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A drawing of his smiling face with a birthday cake
answer_options.B: An audio recording of himself reading the story aloud
answer_options.C: A photo of his dog sleeping on the couch
answer_options.D: A picture of the colorful balloons and presents at his party

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because a drawing of his smiling face with the cake is a visual display that clarifies his happy feelings from the party. Option B is correct because an audio recording of himself reading the story aloud helps clarify his ideas and allows the audience to hear the story clearly. Option D is correct because a picture of the balloons and presents is a visual display that clarifies what his party looked like. Option C is incorrect because a photo of his dog sleeping does not relate to his birthday party story and would not help clarify any ideas, thoughts, or feelings about that experience.
subject: ELA

question: Emma is presenting a recount about her first time hiking up a steep hill. She felt tired and wanted to quit halfway up, but she felt proud when she reached the top. She wants to add visual displays to clarify her feelings for the audience. Which visual displays should Emma add? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A drawing of herself looking tired while sitting on a rock halfway up the hill
answer_options.B: A map showing the path from the bottom to the top of the hill
answer_options.C: A drawing of herself smiling with her arms raised at the top of the hill
answer_options.D: A picture of the lunch she ate after finishing the hike

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because a drawing showing Emma looking tired clarifies the feeling of exhaustion and wanting to quit that she describes halfway through her recount. Option C is correct because a drawing of her smiling with her arms raised at the top clarifies the proud feeling she had when she succeeded. Option B is incorrect because a map shows the route and location (an idea) but does not visually show or clarify the specific feelings Emma experienced during the hike. Option D is incorrect because a picture of her lunch is unrelated to the feelings she had while hiking up the hill.
subject: ELA

question: Liam is sharing a story about his birthday party at the zoo. He wants to help his classmates understand how excited he felt when he saw the giraffes. Which additions would clarify his ideas and feelings? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: A drawing of the tall giraffe eating leaves from a tree
answer_options.B: An audio recording where he uses an excited voice when describing the giraffes
answer_options.C: A photograph showing him smiling next to the giraffe exhibit
answer_options.D: A poster listing facts about how much giraffes weigh and what they eat

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because adding a drawing of the giraffe creates a visual display that helps the audience see what Liam saw, clarifying his experience. Option B is correct because creating an audio recording with an excited voice helps convey the feelings he had during the party, clarifying his emotions. Option C is correct because including a photograph provides a visual display that shows his actual smile at the exhibit, clarifying his happy feelings. Option D is incorrect because listing facts about giraffes does not help clarify Liam's personal ideas, thoughts, or feelings about his birthday experience; it only provides general information unrelated to his recount.
subject: ELA

question: A teacher asks, "What happened to your library book?" Which responses are complete sentences that answer the teacher's question? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I left it on the bus.
answer_options.B: It is at home on my desk.
answer_options.C: On the kitchen table.
answer_options.D: Lost.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject (I) and a verb (left), and it provides the requested detail about where the book is. Option B is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject (It) and a verb (is), and it provides the requested detail. Option C is incorrect because it is a sentence fragment (prepositional phrase) that lacks a subject and a verb. Option D is incorrect because it is a sentence fragment (single word) that lacks a subject and a verb.
subject: ELA

question: During show-and-tell, your classmate asks, "How did you make your clay bowl so smooth?" You should answer using complete sentences that give specific details about your process. Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I used water on my fingers to smooth the clay.
answer_options.B: Rubbing the clay with a little bit of water.
answer_options.C: The bowl is smooth and round.
answer_options.D: My teacher helped me roll the clay flat first.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject (I) and a verb (used), and it provides the requested detail about the specific process used to smooth the clay. Option D is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject (My teacher) and a verb (helped), and it clarifies how the bowl was made. Option B is incorrect because it is a sentence fragment; it lacks a subject and a main verb, so it is not a complete sentence. Option C is incorrect because, although it is grammatically a complete sentence, it does not answer the question or provide the requested clarification about how the bowl was made; it only describes the appearance of the finished product.
subject: ELA

question: A teacher asks, 'What happened at the park yesterday?' Which responses are complete sentences that give the requested detail? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I played on the swings with my sister.
answer_options.B: Running around the big oak tree.
answer_options.C: The dog chased a squirrel up the fence.
answer_options.D: A sunny day at the park.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it is a complete sentence containing a subject ('I') and a predicate ('played on the swings with my sister') that provides the detail the teacher requested. Option C is correct because it is a complete sentence with a subject ('The dog') and a predicate ('chased a squirrel up the fence') that also answers the question with specific information. Option B is incorrect because it is a sentence fragment; it describes an action but lacks a subject to tell who was running. Option D is incorrect because it is also a fragment; it names a thing ('A sunny day at the park') but lacks a verb telling what happened, so it does not form a complete sentence.
subject: ELA

question: Emma wrote an opinion piece about why she likes summer. Which sentences from her writing use a linking word to connect an opinion to a reason? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I love summer because the days are long and sunny.
answer_options.B: Summer comes after spring and before fall.
answer_options.C: I think summer is the best season, and I can swim every day.
answer_options.D: It is hot in the summer.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses the linking word 'because' to connect the opinion 'I love summer' to the reason 'the days are long and sunny.' Option B is incorrect because it states a fact about when summer occurs and does not express an opinion or use a linking word. Option C is correct because it uses the linking word 'and' to connect the opinion 'I think summer is the best season' to the reason 'I can swim every day.' Option D is incorrect because it states a fact about summer weather without expressing an opinion or using a linking word to connect an opinion to a reason.
subject: ELA

question:
Read these sentences from Jada's opinion piece about why dogs make good pets.

'Dogs make good pets because they like to play. They can catch balls and run fast. Dogs are also good at keeping you safe. Everyone should get a dog.'

Which sentences use a linking word to connect the opinion to a reason? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Dogs make good pets because they like to play.
answer_options.B: They can catch balls and run fast.
answer_options.C: Dogs are also good at keeping you safe.
answer_options.D: Everyone should get a dog.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses the linking word 'because' to connect the opinion (that dogs make good pets) to the reason (that they like to play). Option C is correct because it uses the linking word 'also' to add another reason (keeping you safe) that supports the opinion. Option B is incorrect because even though it contains the word 'and,' it only connects two actions (catching balls and running fast) inside one sentence; it does not use a linking word to connect the opinion to a reason. Option D is incorrect because it is a concluding statement that tells the reader what to do, not a reason connected to the opinion by a linking word.
subject: ELA

question:
Read this short paragraph a student wrote about their favorite season.

"Fall is the best season because the leaves change beautiful colors. Also, you can pick apples at the orchard. I love wearing cozy sweaters when it gets chilly. That is why fall is my favorite time of year."

Which sentences show the student using parts of a good opinion piece? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Fall is the best season because the leaves change beautiful colors.
answer_options.B: Also, you can pick apples at the orchard.
answer_options.C: I love wearing cozy sweaters when it gets chilly.
answer_options.D: That is why fall is my favorite time of year.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it states the writer's opinion ('Fall is the best season') and uses the linking word 'because' to connect the opinion to a supporting reason. Option B is correct because it supplies another reason and uses the linking word 'Also' to connect it to the opinion and previous reasons. Option D is correct because it provides a concluding statement that wraps up the opinion piece. Option C is incorrect because although it gives a detail about fall, it does not use a linking word (such as 'and' or 'also') to connect this reason to the writer's opinion or to the other reasons, which is a key part of writing a good opinion piece.
subject: ELA

question: Tom is writing an informative report about butterflies. Which sentences show that Tom is using facts, definitions, or a conclusion in his report? Select all that apply.

answer_options.A: Butterflies are insects that go through a change called metamorphosis.
answer_options.B: Butterfly eggs are usually laid on green leaves.
answer_options.C: Butterflies are the prettiest insects in the whole world.
answer_options.D: Now you know how butterflies grow and change.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it provides a definition (metamorphosis) to develop the topic. Option B is correct because it provides a factual detail about where butterfly eggs are laid. Option D is correct because it provides a concluding statement that wraps up the report. Option C is incorrect because it states a personal opinion ('prettiest') rather than a fact or definition suitable for an informative text.
subject: ELA

question:
A student wrote this paragraph about butterflies:

"Butterflies are insects with colorful wings. Metamorphosis is a big change that butterflies go through. A caterpillar forms a chrysalis around its body. This is how a butterfly grows."

Which sentences use facts or definitions to develop points about butterflies? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Butterflies are insects with colorful wings.
answer_options.B: Metamorphosis is a big change that butterflies go through.
answer_options.C: A caterpillar forms a chrysalis around its body.
answer_options.D: This is how a butterfly grows.

answer_explanation: Option B is correct because it provides a definition of metamorphosis, which helps develop the point about how butterflies change form. Option C is correct because it provides a specific fact about the chrysalis stage, which develops the point about the butterfly life cycle. Option A is incorrect because it introduces the general topic rather than developing a specific point with facts or definitions. Option D is incorrect because it serves as a concluding statement that wraps up the paragraph, not as a fact or definition that develops points.
subject: ELA

question: Sara is writing an informative text about butterflies for her class. Which sentences should she use to help her report? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Butterflies go through a change called metamorphosis.
answer_options.B: Butterflies are insects with colorful wings and six legs.
answer_options.C: These insects are important pollinators in our gardens.
answer_options.D: I caught a butterfly with my net on Saturday.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses a definition (explaining what metamorphosis is) to develop a point about how butterflies grow. Option B is correct because it introduces the topic by telling what butterflies are and describing their features. Option C is correct because it provides a concluding statement that wraps up why butterflies matter. Option D is incorrect because it tells a personal story using 'I' and 'caught,' which belongs in a narrative text rather than an informative report.
subject: ELA

question: Read these short stories. Which ones tell a sequence of events, use time-order words (like first or then), and describe how someone felt? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: First, I woke up. Then I ate breakfast. I was happy because it was Saturday. Finally, I went to the park.
answer_options.B: I like dogs. They are fluffy. Dogs run fast.
answer_options.C: Yesterday I lost my tooth. I felt scared at first. Then the dentist helped me. In the end, I was brave.
answer_options.D: The sun is hot. It shines bright. I see birds.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses time-order words (First, Then, Finally) to show the sequence of events, describes actions (woke up, ate breakfast, went) and feelings (happy), and provides a sense of closure. Option C is correct because it uses temporal words (at first, Then, In the end) to signal the order of events, includes details about feelings (scared, brave) and actions (lost my tooth, dentist helped), and gives the story an ending. Option B is incorrect because it does not tell a sequence of events or use time-order words; it simply lists facts about dogs without a narrative structure. Option D is incorrect because it describes observations without a story sequence, lacks temporal words to show order, and does not provide narrative closure.
subject: ELA

question: Read these four short story drafts. Which drafts use time-order words (like first, next, or finally) to show the order of events, include details that tell what the character felt or thought, AND provide an ending that gives a sense of closure? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: First, I heard a loud noise under my bed. I felt scared that a monster was hiding there. Then I looked and saw my cat playing with a toy. Finally, I laughed and felt happy that it was only Luna.
answer_options.B: Next, we walked to the ice cream shop in town. I was excited to pick my favorite flavor. After that, I ate two scoops on a cone. At last, we walked home together in the warm sun.
answer_options.C: My tower of blocks was very tall and colorful. It crashed to the floor with a bang. I picked up all the pieces and put them in the bin.
answer_options.D: The rain started falling from the gray sky. I opened my red umbrella. I walked to school and sat at my desk.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it uses time-order words (First, Then, Finally), describes the character's feelings (felt scared, felt happy), and ends with a sense of closure (laughed and felt happy). Option B is correct because it uses time-order words (Next, After that, At last), describes feelings (excited), and provides closure (At last, we walked home). Option C is incorrect because it lacks time-order words to signal the sequence of events and does not describe what the character felt or thought. Option D is incorrect because it does not use time-order words, does not describe feelings or thoughts, and ends abruptly without a sense of closure.
subject: ELA

question: Read these sentences from students' stories. Which sentences include specific details that tell what a character is thinking, doing, or feeling? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: I wondered if Mom would remember my birthday.
answer_options.B: We had lunch.
answer_options.C: My heart beat fast when I opened the door.
answer_options.D: It was a Saturday.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because it includes a specific detail about what the character is thinking ('wondered if Mom would remember'). Option C is correct because it describes a physical feeling ('heart beat fast') and a specific action ('opened the door') that show how the character feels. Option B is incorrect because it only states a basic action without elaboration or detail about how the action was performed or how the character felt. Option D is incorrect because it only provides setting information (the day of the week) and does not describe any character's actions, thoughts, or feelings.
subject: ELA

question: Mia wrote a story about her pet dog. She wants to revise and edit her writing to focus on her topic and make it stronger. Which actions will help Mia improve her story? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Add a sentence that tells what her dog likes to eat.
answer_options.B: Fix the spelling of the word 'wlk' to 'walk'.
answer_options.C: Write a new sentence about her favorite ice cream flavor.
answer_options.D: Cross out a sentence that talks about her grandma's car.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because adding details about what the dog likes to eat strengthens the writing about the topic. Option B is correct because fixing the misspelled word 'wlk' is editing that makes the writing clearer and stronger. Option D is correct because removing a sentence about grandma's car helps the story stay focused on the topic of her pet dog. Option C is incorrect because writing about ice cream adds information that is off-topic and does not help the story stay focused on her pet dog.
subject: ELA

question: Read the draft about planting a garden. '1. I planted seeds in the dirt. 2. my mom helped me water them. 3. we saw a movie last weekend. 4. The plants grew tall and green. 5. I love chocolate ice cream.' The writer wants to focus on the topic and strengthen the writing. Which changes should the writer make? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Remove sentence 3 about the movie.
answer_options.B: Capitalize the first letter in sentence 2.
answer_options.C: Remove sentence 5 about ice cream.
answer_options.D: Remove sentence 1 about planting seeds.

answer_explanation: A is correct because removing sentence 3 eliminates information about seeing a movie, which is off-topic for a garden story and helps keep the writing focused. B is correct because sentence 2 begins with a lowercase letter and needs capitalization to follow editing rules and strengthen the writing. C is correct because removing sentence 5 about ice cream keeps the writing focused on the garden topic.
subject: ELA

question: Liam wrote a story about his trip to the zoo. He wants to make his writing better and stay on topic. Which steps should Liam take? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Add more details about the lions he saw at the zoo.
answer_options.B: Check and fix any spelling mistakes in his story.
answer_options.C: Write a new paragraph about his favorite superhero.
answer_options.D: Remove the sentence about what he ate for breakfast.

answer_explanation: Option A is correct because adding details about the zoo animals strengthens the writing by revising to make it more interesting while staying on the topic of the zoo trip. Option B is correct because checking and fixing spelling mistakes is editing, which strengthens the writing by making it clearer and easier to read. Option C is incorrect because writing about superheroes is off-topic; Liam is writing about the zoo, so adding superhero content would not help him focus on his topic. Option D is correct because removing the sentence about breakfast (which is not about the zoo) helps Liam focus his writing on the topic of his zoo trip through revising.
subject: ELA

question: Which activities show a student using digital tools to write or share their work? Select ALL that apply.

answer_options.A: Using a computer to type a story with the teacher's help.
answer_options.B: Working with a classmate to post a book review on the class website.
